title: Utah Aspire Plus Math Grade 9 Practice Bank

metadata.description: Original Utah Aspire Plus-aligned Grade 9 / Secondary Math I practice bank. 10 topics, 30 questions per topic, 5 difficulty levels.
sourceAlignment: Utah Aspire Plus Grade 9 mathematics blueprint
Utah Secondary Mathematics I course overview
ACT Aspire Early High School exemplar mathematics items
metadata.notes: Questions are original practice items written to match Utah Aspire Plus Grade 9 / Secondary Math I skills and style. They are not official Utah Aspire Plus items.

topics.name: Numbers, Rates, and Percents
topics.alignment: Utah Aspire Plus Grade 9 / Secondary Math I foundations: numerical reasoning, ratios, rates, and percent modeling

questions.question: A store advertises a 25% discount on a $64 backpack. What is the amount of the discount?
A trail is 3/4 mile long, and a runner adds another 1/2 mile warm-up. What total distance does the runner cover?
If 5 notebooks cost $12.50, what is the cost of 1 notebook?
questions.explanation: Divide total cost by number of notebooks: 12.50 ÷ 5 = 2.50.

questions.question: What is 3/4 + 5/8 ?
What is 25% of 64?
questions.explanation: 25% means 1/4. One fourth of 64 is 16.

questions.question: A car travels 210 miles in 3.5 hours. What is its average speed?
questions.explanation: Average speed is distance divided by time: 210 ÷ 3.5 = 60 miles per hour.

questions.question: At sunrise the temperature was -6°F. By noon it had risen 14°F. What was the temperature at noon?
questions.explanation: Start at -6 and add 14: -6 + 14 = 8.

questions.question: Which of the following is equal to 0.00056 ?
questions.explanation: Move the decimal 4 places right to get 5.6, so the exponent is -4.

questions.question: Solve the proportion x/7 = 18/21.
questions.explanation: 18/21 simplifies to 6/7, so x/7 = 6/7 and x = 6.

questions.question: What is 2 1/3 - 5/6 ?
questions.explanation: Convert 2 1/3 to 7/3, then to sixths: 14/6 - 5/6 = 9/6 = 3/2 = 1 1/2.

questions.question: A sweatshirt costs $80 before a 20% discount. What is the sale price?
questions.explanation: A 20% discount is 0.20 × 80 = 16 dollars. Subtract: 80 - 16 = 64.

questions.question: On a map, 1 inch represents 12 miles. Two towns are 3.5 inches apart on the map. How far apart are they in reality?
questions.explanation: Multiply 3.5 inches by 12 miles per inch: 3.5 × 12 = 42 miles.

questions.question: A calculator costs $48 before tax. If the sales tax rate is 7.5%, what is the total cost?
Which number is greatest?
questions.explanation: Convert to decimals: 5/8 = 0.625, 0.61 = 0.61, 63% = 0.63, and 0.6 = 0.60. The greatest is 63%.

questions.question: A car travels 210 miles in 3.5 hours. What is the car’s average speed?
questions.explanation: Average speed is distance ÷ time = 210 ÷ 3.5 = 60 mph.

questions.question: If 18 is 30% of a number, what is the number?
questions.explanation: Set up 0.30x = 18. Divide by 0.30 to get x = 60.

questions.question: A purchase total is $53.50 after 7% sales tax. What was the price before tax?
questions.explanation: Let p be the pre-tax price. Then 1.07p = 53.50, so p = 53.50 / 1.07 = 50.

questions.question: A printer makes 180 pages in 12 minutes. At the same rate, how many minutes are needed to print 255 pages?
questions.explanation: The rate is 180 ÷ 12 = 15 pages per minute. Then 255 ÷ 15 = 17 minutes.

questions.question: A jacket originally costs $50. Its price is increased by 20% and then discounted by 10%. What is the final price?
questions.explanation: After a 20% increase, the price is 50 × 1.2 = 60. Then 10% off gives 60 × 0.9 = 54.

questions.question: Suppose y varies directly with x and y = 10 when x = 4. What is y when x = 7?
A backpack is marked down 20% and then another 10%. If the original price is $150, what is the final price?
questions.explanation: After 20% off, the price is 0.80 × 150 = 120. After another 10% off, the price is 0.90 × 120 = 108.

questions.question: Three liters of 20% acid are mixed with two liters of 35% acid. What is the concentration of the mixture?
questions.explanation: There are 0.20(3) = 0.6 liters of acid in the first mixture and 0.35(2) = 0.7 liters in the second, for a total of 1.3 liters in 5 liters. That is 1.3/5 = 0.26 = 26%.

questions.question: A price decreases by 20% and then increases by 25%. Compared with the original price, the final price is:
questions.explanation: Use a convenient starting value such as 100. After a 20% decrease: 80. After a 25% increase: 80 × 1.25 = 100.

questions.question: What is (3/4) ÷ (5/8) ?
questions.explanation: Divide by multiplying by the reciprocal: (3/4) × (8/5) = 24/20 = 6/5.

questions.question: A school’s student-to-teacher ratio is 18:1. If the school has 756 students, how many teachers are there?
questions.explanation: Let t be the number of teachers. 18 students per teacher means 18t = 756, so t = 42.

questions.question: An exam average uses 40% from Test 1 and 60% from Test 2. If a student scores 78 on Test 1 and 86 on Test 2, what is the weighted average?

questions.skill: original price after discount and tax
questions.question: After a 20% discount and then 7.5% sales tax, a jacket costs $86. What was the original price to the nearest cent?
questions.explanation: Let p be the original price. After discount and tax, the price is 1.075 × 0.80p = 0.86p. Since 0.86p = 86, p = 100.

questions.question: An exchange booth charges a 3% fee. If $242.50 is exchanged for euros after the fee, how many dollars were exchanged before the fee?
questions.explanation: After a 3% fee, 97% of the original amount remains. Let x be the original amount: 0.97x = 242.50, so x = 250.

topics.name: Expressions and Exponents
topics.alignment: Utah Aspire Plus Grade 9 / Secondary Math I algebra foundations

questions.question: Evaluate 3a - 2b when a = 4 and b = 1.
Which expression is equivalent to 5x + 3x - 7 ?
questions.explanation: Combine like terms: 5x + 3x = 8x.

questions.question: Which expression is equivalent to 2(y + 4) - 3 ?
questions.explanation: Distribute 2 to get 2y + 8, then subtract 3 to get 2y + 5.

questions.question: Simplify: 3x + 5x - 2
questions.explanation: Combine like terms: 3x + 5x = 8x, so the expression simplifies to 8x - 2.

questions.question: Evaluate 2a - 3b when a = 4 and b = -2.
questions.explanation: Substitute the values: 2(4) - 3(-2) = 8 + 6 = 14.

questions.question: Simplify: 4(2y - 3) + 5
questions.explanation: Distribute 4 to get 8y - 12, then add 5: 8y - 12 + 5 = 8y - 7.

questions.question: Simplify (2x^3)(3x^2).
questions.explanation: Multiply coefficients and add exponents of x: 2·3 = 6 and x^3·x^2 = x^5.

questions.question: Simplify 4m - 3(2m - 5).
questions.explanation: Distribute -3: 4m - 6m + 15 = -2m + 15.

questions.question: What is 2^3 · 2^4 ?
questions.explanation: Add the exponents: 2^(3+4) = 2^7 = 128.

questions.question: Simplify: 5x - 2(3x - 4)
questions.explanation: Distribute -2: 5x - 6x + 8 = -x + 8.

questions.question: Simplify: x^2 · x^5 ÷ x^3
questions.explanation: Add exponents when multiplying and subtract when dividing: x^(2+5-3) = x^4.

questions.question: Write 0.00056 in scientific notation.
questions.explanation: Move the decimal 4 places to the right to get 5.6, so the exponent is -4.

questions.question: Simplify ((3x^2y)(2xy^3)) / (6xy).
questions.explanation: The numerator is 6x^3y^4. Dividing by 6xy leaves x^2y^3.

questions.question: If x^-2 = 1/25 and x > 0, what is x ?
questions.explanation: x^-2 = 1/x^2. So 1/x^2 = 1/25, which means x^2 = 25 and x = 5.

questions.question: Which expression is equivalent to (a + b)^2 - ab ?
questions.explanation: Expand (a + b)^2 = a^2 + 2ab + b^2, then subtract ab to get a^2 + ab + b^2.

questions.question: Simplify: (2x^3y^2)(3x^2y^-1)
questions.explanation: Multiply coefficients 2·3 = 6. Add exponents on like bases: x^(3+2) = x^5 and y^(2-1) = y.

questions.question: Simplify: (5a^0b^3)/(a^-2b)
questions.explanation: Since a^0 = 1, the expression is 5b^3 ÷ (a^-2b). Dividing by a^-2 is the same as multiplying by a^2, and b^3 ÷ b = b^2.

questions.question: Evaluate: (2/3)^-2
questions.explanation: A negative exponent means take the reciprocal and then square: (2/3)^-2 = (3/2)^2 = 9/4.

questions.question: Simplify (9p^4q^2)/(3pq^-1).
questions.explanation: Divide coefficients: 9/3 = 3. Subtract exponents: p^(4-1)=p^3 and q^(2-(-1))=q^3.

questions.question: If 3^(x+1) = 81, what is x ?
Solve for m in n = mp^2 / q.
questions.explanation: Multiply both sides by q to get nq = mp^2, then divide by p^2.

questions.question: Simplify: ((x^3y^-2)^2 · x^-1) / y
questions.explanation: First square: (x^3y^-2)^2 = x^6y^-4. Multiply by x^-1 to get x^5y^-4, then divide by y to get x^5y^-5 = x^5 / y^5.

questions.question: Multiply and write in scientific notation: (3 × 10^5)(2 × 10^-3)
questions.explanation: Multiply the coefficients to get 6 and add the exponents: 10^(5 + (-3)) = 10^2.

questions.question: Simplify: (-2m^2n^3)^2
questions.explanation: Squaring removes the negative sign and doubles each exponent: (-2)^2 = 4, m^(2·2) = m^4, and n^(3·2) = n^6.

questions.question: Simplify (2x^-1y^2)^2 / (4y).
questions.explanation: (2x^-1y^2)^2 = 4x^-2y^4. Dividing by 4y gives x^-2y^3 = y^3/x^2.

questions.question: If a + b = 5 and ab = 6, what is a^2 + b^2 ?
Which expression is equivalent to (x^3 - x^2)/x^2 for x ≠ 0 ?
questions.explanation: Factor x^2 from the numerator: x^2(x - 1). Dividing by x^2 leaves x - 1.

questions.question: Simplify: ((2x^-2y^3)^3) / (4x^-1y)
questions.explanation: First expand the numerator: (2x^-2y^3)^3 = 8x^-6y^9. Now divide by 4x^-1y to get 2x^(-6-(-1))y^(9-1) = 2x^-5y^8 = 2y^8 / x^5.

questions.question: Translate and simplify: 'three less than twice a number, all squared' for x = 5.
questions.explanation: The phrase means (2x - 3)^2. Substitute x = 5: (10 - 3)^2 = 7^2 = 49.

questions.question: Evaluate 2a^2b^-1 when a = -3 and b = 1/2.
questions.explanation: Compute a^2 = 9 and b^-1 = 2. Then 2 · 9 · 2 = 36.

topics.name: Linear Equations
topics.alignment: Utah Aspire Plus Grade 9 / Secondary Math I algebra

questions.question: Solve x + 7 = 19.
questions.explanation: Subtract 7 from both sides to get x = 12.

questions.question: Solve 4x = 28.
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by 4.

questions.question: Solve x/3 = 5.
questions.explanation: Multiply both sides by 3.

questions.question: Solve: x + 7 = 15
questions.explanation: Subtract 7 from both sides to get x = 8.

questions.question: Solve: 3x = 18
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by 3: x = 6.

questions.question: Solve: x - 4 = -9
questions.explanation: Add 4 to both sides: x = -5.

questions.question: Solve 5x - 8 = 17.
questions.explanation: Add 8 to both sides and divide by 5: 5x = 25, so x = 5.

questions.question: Solve 2(x + 3) = 18.
questions.explanation: Divide by 2 to get x + 3 = 9, then subtract 3.

questions.question: Solve 3x + 4 = x + 18.
questions.explanation: Subtract x from both sides and subtract 4: 2x = 14, so x = 7.

questions.question: Solve: 5x - 7 = 18
questions.explanation: Add 7 to both sides to get 5x = 25, then divide by 5.

questions.question: Solve: 3(x - 2) = 21
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by 3 to get x - 2 = 7, then add 2.

questions.question: Solve: 7 - 2x = 19
questions.explanation: Subtract 7 from both sides: -2x = 12. Divide by -2 to get x = -6.

questions.question: Solve (x - 2)/3 = 5.
questions.explanation: Multiply both sides by 3: x - 2 = 15, so x = 17.

questions.question: Solve 0.4x + 6 = 14.
questions.explanation: Subtract 6: 0.4x = 8. Then x = 8/0.4 = 20.

questions.question: Solve 7 - 2(x - 1) = 15.
questions.explanation: Distribute: 7 - 2x + 2 = 15, so 9 - 2x = 15. Then -2x = 6, so x = -3.

questions.question: Solve: 2(x + 3) - 4 = 3x + 1
questions.explanation: Simplify the left side: 2x + 2 = 3x + 1. Subtract 2x to get 2 = x + 1, so x = 1.

questions.question: Solve: x/4 + 5 = 11
questions.explanation: Subtract 5 from both sides to get x/4 = 6, then multiply by 4.

questions.question: Solve: (3/5)x - 4 = 8
questions.explanation: Add 4 to both sides: (3/5)x = 12. Multiply by 5/3 to get x = 20.

questions.question: Solve (3/4)x - 5 = 7.
questions.explanation: Add 5 to get (3/4)x = 12. Multiply by 4/3 to get x = 16.

questions.question: Solve 2(3y - 4) - 5(y + 1) = 7.
questions.explanation: Expand: 6y - 8 - 5y - 5 = 7, so y - 13 = 7. Then y = 20.

questions.question: A phone plan charges a $25 monthly fee plus $0.10 per text. If the monthly bill is $41.50, how many texts were sent?
Solve for b in A = (1/2)(b + h)k.
questions.explanation: Multiply both sides by 2/k to get 2A/k = b + h. Then subtract h.

questions.question: Solve for t in d = rt.
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by r to isolate t.

questions.question: Solve: 0.3(x - 4) = 1.2x + 2.4
questions.explanation: Distribute 0.3 to get 0.3x - 1.2 = 1.2x + 2.4. Subtract 0.3x and subtract 2.4: -3.6 = 0.9x, so x = -4.

questions.question: Solve A = (1/2)h(b1 + b2) for h.
questions.explanation: Multiply both sides by 2, then divide by (b1 + b2).

questions.question: Solve 3(x - 2)/5 + 1 = x/2.
questions.explanation: Multiply by 10: 6(x - 2) + 10 = 5x. Then 6x - 12 + 10 = 5x, so x = 2.

questions.question: Three consecutive integers have a sum of 72. What is the middle integer?
questions.explanation: Let the integers be n - 1, n, and n + 1. Their sum is 3n = 72, so n = 24.

questions.question: A gym charges a $15 sign-up fee plus $8 per class. If a member pays $63 total, how many classes did the member attend?
questions.explanation: Write 15 + 8c = 63. Then 8c = 48, so c = 6.

questions.question: The perimeter of a rectangle is 46 feet. Its length is 5 feet more than twice its width. What is the width?
questions.explanation: Let w be the width. Then the length is 2w + 5, and 2[(2w + 5) + w] = 46. This becomes 6w + 10 = 46, so w = 6.

questions.question: A concert organizer sells student tickets for $8 each and collects $504 from ticket sales. If no other ticket types are sold, how many student tickets were sold?
questions.explanation: Let x be the number of tickets. Then 8x = 504, so x = 63.

topics.name: Linear Inequalities and Absolute Value
topics.alignment: Utah Aspire Plus Grade 9 / Secondary Math I algebra

questions.question: Solve x - 4 > 9.
questions.explanation: Add 4 to both sides.

questions.question: Solve 3x ≤ 21.
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by 3.

questions.question: If |x| = 5, which of the following could be x ?
questions.explanation: A number with absolute value 5 can be 5 or −5, so 5 is one possible value.

questions.question: Solve: x + 3 > 7
questions.explanation: Subtract 3 from both sides to get x > 4.

questions.question: Solve: 2x ≤ 10
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by 2. The inequality direction stays the same because 2 is positive.

questions.question: Solve: -x < 4
questions.explanation: Multiply both sides by -1 and reverse the inequality: x > -4.

questions.question: Solve 2x - 3 < 7.
questions.explanation: Add 3 to both sides to get 2x < 10, then divide by 2.

questions.question: Solve -4x ≥ 12.
questions.explanation: Divide by -4 and reverse the inequality sign.

questions.question: Solve |x - 2| = 6.
questions.explanation: Set x - 2 = 6 or x - 2 = -6. The solutions are 8 and -4.

questions.question: Solve: 3x - 5 ≥ 10
questions.explanation: Add 5 to both sides: 3x ≥ 15. Divide by 3: x ≥ 5.

questions.question: Solve: 2 - 4x > 14
questions.explanation: Subtract 2: -4x > 12. Divide by -4 and reverse the inequality: x < -3.

questions.question: Solve: 5(x - 1) ≤ 20
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by 5 to get x - 1 ≤ 4, then add 1.

questions.question: Solve 5 ≤ 2x + 1 < 11.
questions.explanation: Subtract 1: 4 ≤ 2x < 10. Divide by 2: 2 ≤ x < 5.

questions.question: Solve |3y| = 12.
Which interval represents the solutions of |x| < 3 ?
questions.explanation: A number has absolute value less than 3 if it lies between -3 and 3.

questions.question: Solve: -2 < 3x + 1 ≤ 10
questions.explanation: Subtract 1 from all three parts: -3 < 3x ≤ 9. Divide by 3 to get -1 < x ≤ 3.

questions.question: Solve: |x - 4| = 7
questions.explanation: An absolute value equation splits into two cases: x - 4 = 7 or x - 4 = -7. Solving gives x = 11 or x = -3.

questions.question: Solve: |2x + 1| < 5
questions.explanation: Write the compound inequality -5 < 2x + 1 < 5. Subtract 1 to get -6 < 2x < 4, then divide by 2.

questions.question: Solve 4 - 2x > 10.
questions.explanation: Subtract 4: -2x > 6. Divide by -2 and reverse the sign: x < -3.

questions.question: Solve |x + 1| ≤ 4.
questions.explanation: Write -4 ≤ x + 1 ≤ 4 and subtract 1: -5 ≤ x ≤ 3.

questions.question: Adult tickets cost $12 each. Student tickets cost $8 each. If 20 student tickets are sold, how many adult tickets are needed for the total revenue to be at least $320?
questions.explanation: Revenue is 12a + 8(20) ≥ 320, so 12a + 160 ≥ 320. Then 12a ≥ 160, so a ≥ 13.33.... At least 14 adult tickets are needed.

questions.question: Solve: 4 - 2(x + 3) > x - 5
questions.explanation: Distribute: 4 - 2x - 6 > x - 5, so -2x - 2 > x - 5. Add 2x and add 5 to get 3 > 3x, so x < 1.

questions.question: Solve: (3/4)x - 2 ≥ 1/2
questions.explanation: Add 2 to both sides: (3/4)x ≥ 5/2. Multiply by 4/3 to get x ≥ 10/3.

questions.question: Solve: |x + 2| ≥ 6
questions.explanation: For |x + 2| ≥ 6, solve x + 2 ≥ 6 or x + 2 ≤ -6. That gives x ≥ 4 or x ≤ -8.

questions.question: Solve |2x - 5| = 9.
questions.explanation: Set 2x - 5 = 9 or 2x - 5 = -9. The solutions are x = 7 and x = -2.

questions.question: An elevator can carry at most 1,600 pounds. Eight students weigh 140 pounds each, and a teacher weighs 170 pounds. Each box weighs 12 pounds. What is the greatest number of boxes the elevator can carry?
questions.explanation: Current weight is 8(140) + 170 = 1,290. That leaves 310 pounds. Since 310/12 = 25.83..., the maximum whole number of boxes is 25.

questions.question: Solve |x - 4| > 7.
questions.explanation: Either x - 4 > 7 or x - 4 < -7. So x > 11 or x < -3.

questions.question: A tutoring center charges a $25 registration fee plus $12 per session. If a student can spend at most $180, what is the greatest whole number of sessions the student can take?
questions.explanation: Write 25 + 12s ≤ 180. Then 12s ≤ 155, so s ≤ 12.91. The greatest whole number of sessions is 12.

questions.question: Scores on four quizzes are 80, 84, 76, and 90. What score is needed on the fifth quiz to earn an average of at least 82?
questions.explanation: Set up (80 + 84 + 76 + 90 + x)/5 ≥ 82. The known sum is 330, so 330 + x ≥ 410, which gives x ≥ 80.

questions.question: A business models profit by P(x) = 18x - 450, where x is the number of units sold. For what values of x is the profit positive?
questions.explanation: Positive profit means 18x - 450 > 0. Add 450 and divide by 18 to get x > 25.

topics.name: Systems of Equations and Modeling
topics.alignment: Utah Aspire Plus Grade 9 / Secondary Math I algebra and modeling

questions.question: If x = 3 and y = 2x + 1, what is y ?
questions.explanation: Substitute x = 3 into y = 2x + 1 to get y = 7.

questions.question: For the system x + y = 10 and x - y = 2, what is x ?
questions.explanation: Add the equations: 2x = 12, so x = 6.

questions.question: At a snack bar, 2 drinks and 1 pretzel cost $7. If a pretzel costs $3, how much does a drink cost?
questions.explanation: Subtract the pretzel cost: 2 drinks cost $4, so one drink costs $2.

questions.question: Solve the system: x + y = 11 and x - y = 3.
questions.explanation: Add the equations: 2x = 14, so x = 7. Then y = 11 - 7 = 4.

questions.question: Solve the system: 2x + y = 9 and x - y = 0.
questions.explanation: From x - y = 0, we get x = y. Substitute into 2x + y = 9 to get 3x = 9, so x = 3 and y = 3.

questions.question: Solve the system: x + 2y = 12 and x = y + 3.
questions.explanation: Substitute x = y + 3 into x + 2y = 12: y + 3 + 2y = 12. Then 3y = 9, so y = 3 and x = 6.

questions.question: Solve the system 2x + y = 11 and x - y = 1.
questions.explanation: From x - y = 1, y = x - 1. Substitute: 2x + (x - 1) = 11, so x = 4 and y = 3.

questions.question: The lines y = -x + 5 and y = 2x - 4 intersect at what point?
questions.explanation: Set -x + 5 = 2x - 4. Then 9 = 3x, so x = 3 and y = 2.

questions.question: Adult tickets cost $10 and child tickets cost $6. If 2 tickets cost $16 total, which combination is possible?
questions.explanation: One adult and one child cost 10 + 6 = 16.

questions.question: Solve the system: 2x + y = 7 and x - y = 2.
questions.explanation: Add the equations after rewriting x - y = 2 as x + (-y) = 2. This gives 3x = 9, so x = 3 and y = 1.

questions.question: Solve the system: 4x - y = 11 and 2x + y = 7.
questions.explanation: Add the equations to eliminate y: 6x = 18, so x = 3. Then 2(3) + y = 7 gives y = 1.

questions.question: Solve the system: 5x + 3y = 19 and x + y = 5.
questions.explanation: From x + y = 5, y = 5 - x. Substitute: 5x + 3(5 - x) = 19. Then 2x = 4, so x = 2 and y = 3.

questions.question: Adult tickets cost $8 and student tickets cost $5. If 31 tickets are sold for a total of $203, how many adult tickets are sold?
questions.explanation: Let a + s = 31 and 8a + 5s = 203. Substitute s = 31 - a: 8a + 5(31 - a) = 203, so 3a = 48 and a = 16.

questions.question: The sum of 2 numbers is 19 and their difference is 5. What are the numbers?
questions.explanation: Let x + y = 19 and x - y = 5. Adding gives 2x = 24, so x = 12 and y = 7.

questions.question: Solve the system 3x + 2y = 18 and x - y = 1.
questions.explanation: From x - y = 1, x = y + 1. Substitute: 3(y + 1) + 2y = 18, so 5y = 15 and y = 3. Then x = 4.

questions.question: A car rental company charges a daily fee d dollars and a mileage fee m dollars per mile. A 2-day, 100-mile rental costs $58, and a 4-day, 250-mile rental costs $127. What is the daily fee?
questions.explanation: Write the system 2d + 100m = 58 and 4d + 250m = 127. Solving gives d = 18 and m = 0.22.

questions.question: A jar contains 17 nickels and dimes worth $1.30. How many dimes are in the jar?
questions.explanation: Let n be nickels and d be dimes. Then n + d = 17 and 5n + 10d = 130. Substitute n = 17 - d and solve to get d = 9.

questions.question: A concert sells 120 tickets and collects $900. Adult tickets cost $10 and student tickets cost $5. How many adult tickets were sold?
questions.explanation: Let a be adult tickets and s be student tickets. Then a + s = 120 and 10a + 5s = 900. Divide the second equation by 5 and solve to get a = 60.

questions.question: Gym A charges $35 monthly plus $4 per class. Gym B charges $55 monthly plus $2 per class. After how many classes do the plans cost the same?
questions.explanation: Set 35 + 4c = 55 + 2c. Then 2c = 20, so c = 10.

questions.question: Solve the system 2x + 3y = 19 and 4x - y = 3.
questions.explanation: From 4x - y = 3, y = 4x - 3. Substitute into 2x + 3y = 19: 2x + 3(4x - 3) = 19, so 14x = 28 and x = 2. Then y = 5.

questions.question: A jar contains only quarters and dimes. There are 20 coins worth $3.20 in all. How many quarters are in the jar?
questions.explanation: Let q be quarters and d be dimes. Then q + d = 20 and 25q + 10d = 320. Substitute d = 20 - q: 25q + 200 - 10q = 320, so 15q = 120 and q = 8.

questions.question: What kind of solution does the system have? 2x + 4y = 8 and x + 2y = 5
questions.explanation: Rewrite the first equation as x + 2y = 4. Since x + 2y cannot equal both 4 and 5 at the same time, the lines are parallel and there is no solution.

questions.question: What kind of solution does the system have? 3x - 6y = 9 and x - 2y = 3
questions.explanation: The first equation is just 3 times the second, so they represent the same line.

questions.question: Solve the system: x/2 + y = 5 and x - y = 4.
questions.explanation: From x - y = 4, we get y = x - 4. Substitute into x/2 + y = 5: x/2 + x - 4 = 5. Then 3x/2 = 9, so x = 6 and y = 2.

questions.question: Two stores sell the same type of smoothie. Store A charges $4 per smoothie plus a $6 delivery fee. Store B charges $5 per smoothie plus a $2 delivery fee. For how many smoothies is the total cost the same?
questions.explanation: Set 4s + 6 = 5s + 2. Then 6 = s + 2, so s = 4.

questions.question: Solve the system 4p + 3n = 25 and 2p + n = 11.
questions.explanation: From 2p + n = 11, n = 11 - 2p. Substitute: 4p + 3(11 - 2p) = 25, so -2p = -8 and p = 4. Then n = 3.

questions.question: The perimeter of a rectangle is 34 inches. The length is 3 inches greater than the width. What are the rectangle’s dimensions?
questions.explanation: Let length = l and width = w. Then 2l + 2w = 34 and l = w + 3. Substitute: 2(w + 3) + 2w = 34, so 4w = 28 and w = 7. Then l = 10.

questions.question: Revenue is modeled by R(x) = 12x and cost by C(x) = 200 + 7x. At what number of units is the business at break-even?
questions.explanation: Break-even occurs when revenue equals cost: 12x = 200 + 7x. Then 5x = 200, so x = 40.

questions.question: How many pounds of $10 per pound cashews should be mixed with $4 per pound peanuts to make 30 pounds of a $6 per pound mixture?
questions.explanation: Let c be the pounds of cashews. Then 10c + 4(30 - c) = 6(30). Solving gives 6c = 60, so c = 10.

questions.question: Plan A costs $25 plus $0.05 per text. Plan B costs $10 plus $0.08 per text. At how many texts do the plans cost the same?
questions.explanation: Set the costs equal: 25 + 0.05t = 10 + 0.08t. Then 15 = 0.03t, so t = 500.

topics.name: Functions and Linear Graphs
topics.alignment: Utah Aspire Plus Grade 9 / Secondary Math I functions

questions.question: If f(x) = x + 4, what is f(3)?
questions.explanation: Substitute x = 3 to get 3 + 4 = 7.

questions.question: A line passes through (0, 1) and (2, 5). What is the slope of the line?
Which set of ordered pairs represents a function?
questions.explanation: A relation is a function if each x-value is paired with only one y-value. Only the second set has no repeated x-values with different y-values.

questions.question: If f(x) = 2x + 3, what is f(4)?
questions.explanation: Substitute x = 4 into the rule: 2(4) + 3 = 11.

questions.question: What is the slope of the line through (2, 5) and (6, 13)?
If y = -3x + 1, what is y when x = -2?
If g(x) = 2x^2 - 1, what is g(-2)?
What is the slope of the line through (1, 2) and (4, 11)?
For the equation y = -3x + 6, what is the y-intercept?
questions.explanation: The y-intercept is the value of y when x = 0, which is 6.

questions.question: Which equation represents the line through (0, -3) parallel to y = 2x + 7?
questions.explanation: Parallel lines have the same slope. Since the given line has slope 2 and the new line passes through (0, -3), the equation is y = 2x - 3.

questions.question: A table shows x-values 1, 2, 3 paired with y-values 4, 7, 10. If the pattern is linear, what is y when x = 5?
questions.explanation: The y-values increase by 3 each time, so the rule is y = 3x + 1. Then y = 3(5) + 1 = 16.

questions.question: What is the x-intercept of the line y = 3x - 12?
questions.explanation: At the x-intercept, y = 0. Solve 0 = 3x - 12 to get x = 4.

questions.question: A table shows x-values 0, 1, 2 and corresponding y-values 5, 8, 11. Which equation matches the pattern?
questions.explanation: The y-values increase by 3 each time x increases by 1, so slope is 3. When x = 0, y = 5, so the equation is y = 3x + 5.

questions.question: If f(x) = 1/(x - 4), which value is not in the domain of f?
questions.explanation: The denominator cannot equal 0, so x ≠ 4.

questions.question: If f(x) = |x - 3|, what is f(1)?
If f(x) = x + 2 and g(x) = 3x, what is f(g(4))?
questions.explanation: First compute g(4) = 12, then apply f: f(12) = 12 + 2 = 14.

questions.question: If f(x) = 2x - 5, what is f^-1(11)?
questions.explanation: To invert, solve y = 2x - 5 for x: x = (y + 5)/2. Then f^-1(11) = (11 + 5)/2 = 8.

questions.question: What is the vertex of y = (x - 2)^2 + 1?
questions.explanation: Vertex form is y = (x - h)^2 + k, so the vertex is (h, k) = (2, 1).

questions.question: A line passes through (-2, 4) and (2, -4). Which equation represents the line?
questions.explanation: Slope = (-4 - 4)/(2 - (-2)) = -8/4 = -2. Using point (2, -4), the line is y = -2x.

questions.question: For h(x) = 2(x - 1)^2 + 3, what is the vertex of the parabola?
questions.explanation: In vertex form a(x - h)^2 + k, the vertex is (h, k).

questions.question: If f(x) = x - 2 and g(x) = 3x, what is g(f(5))?
questions.explanation: First find f(5) = 3. Then g(3) = 9.

questions.question: If f(x) = x^2 - 1 and g(x) = x + 3, what is (f ∘ g)(2)?
questions.explanation: Compute g(2) = 5. Then f(5) = 5^2 - 1 = 24.

questions.question: If f(x) = (x - 4)/3, what is f^-1(5)?
For the piecewise function f(x) = 2x + 1 if x < 3 and f(x) = x - 2 if x ≥ 3, what is f(3)?
questions.explanation: Because 3 is in the x ≥ 3 branch, use f(x) = x - 2. Then f(3) = 1.

questions.question: Let f(x) = x + 1 for x < 2 and f(x) = 5 - x for x ≥ 2. What is f(3)?
questions.explanation: Since 3 ≥ 2, use f(x) = 5 - x. Then f(3) = 2.

questions.question: Which equation represents the line through (4, -1) that is perpendicular to y = (1/2)x + 3 ?
questions.explanation: A perpendicular line has slope -2. Using point-slope form with (4, -1): y + 1 = -2(x - 4), so y = -2x + 7.

questions.question: A function passes through (1, 4), (3, 10), and (5, 16). Assuming the function is linear, what is f(8)?
questions.explanation: The change in y is +6 when x increases by 2, so slope is 3. Using (1, 4), the equation is y = 3x + 1. Then f(8) = 25.

questions.question: What is the average rate of change of f(x) = x^2 - 3x from x = 1 to x = 5?
questions.explanation: Compute f(5) = 10 and f(1) = -2. Then average rate of change is (10 - (-2)) / (5 - 1) = 12/4 = 3.

questions.question: Which equation represents the line through (2, -1) and perpendicular to 3x + y = 7?
questions.explanation: Rewrite 3x + y = 7 as y = -3x + 7, so the slope is -3. A perpendicular line has slope 1/3. Use point-slope form through (2, -1) to get y = (1/3)x - 5/3.

questions.question: Which equation is the graph of y = sqrt(x) shifted right 4 units and up 2 units?
questions.explanation: A shift right by 4 changes x to x - 4, and a shift up by 2 adds 2 outside the function.

topics.name: Exponential Relationships
topics.alignment: Utah Aspire Plus Grade 9 / Secondary Math I functions: compare and model exponential growth and decay

questions.question: What is 3^4 ?
If y = 2·3^x, what is y when x = 2 ?
questions.explanation: Substitute x = 2: y = 2·3^2 = 2·9 = 18.

questions.question: A population starts at 40 and triples every year. Which expression models the population after t years?
questions.explanation: Tripling each year means multiply by 3 each time, so the model is 40·3^t.

questions.question: What is 2^5 ?
If y = 3·2^x, what is y when x = 2 ?
questions.explanation: Substitute x = 2: y = 3·4 = 12.

questions.question: For y = 4(3^x), what is the y-intercept?
Solve 2·5^x = 50.
questions.explanation: Divide by 2 to get 5^x = 25 = 5^2, so x = 2.

questions.question: A savings account has $200 and grows by 10% in 1 year. What is the balance after 1 year?
questions.explanation: 10% of 200 is 20, so the new balance is 200 + 20 = 220.

questions.question: A quantity starts at 100 and is cut in half every 2 hours. Which function models the quantity after t hours?
questions.explanation: Half every 2 hours means multiply by 1/2 once for each 2-hour block, so 100(1/2)^(t/2).

questions.question: Solve 5·2^x = 40.
questions.explanation: Divide by 5 to get 2^x = 8 = 2^3, so x = 3.

questions.question: Which function models a quantity that starts at 80 and is cut in half every 3 hours?
questions.explanation: Half every 3 hours means one factor of 1/2 for each 3-hour block, so 80(1/2)^(t/3).

questions.question: A population grows by 6% each year. Which growth factor should be used in an exponential model?
questions.explanation: A 6% increase means multiply by 1 + 0.06 = 1.06.

questions.question: If y = 300(0.8)^t, what is y when t = 2 ?
A table shows x-values 0, 1, 2, 3 and y-values 5, 10, 20, 40. Which rule matches the pattern?
questions.explanation: The output doubles each time x increases by 1, so the rule is y = 5·2^x.

questions.question: A culture starts with 500 bacteria and doubles every 4 hours. How many bacteria are present after 8 hours?
questions.explanation: 8 hours is 2 doubling periods, so 500·2^2 = 2,000.

questions.question: If y = 200(0.9)^t, what is y when t = 2 ?
A table shows x-values 0,1,2,3 and y-values 6,12,24,48. Which equation fits the data?
questions.explanation: The output doubles each time x increases by 1, so y = 6·2^x.

questions.question: A lake has 500 fish and the population triples every 2 years. Which model gives the population after t years?
questions.explanation: Triples every 2 years means one factor of 3 for each 2-year period, so P = 500·3^(t/2).

questions.question: If 4^(x - 1) = 64, what is x ?
questions.explanation: Since 64 = 4^3, we have x - 1 = 3, so x = 4.

questions.question: Quantity A = 50 + 6t and Quantity B = 50(1.08)^t. For very large t, which statement is true?
questions.explanation: An exponential model eventually grows faster than a linear model, so B will eventually exceed A.

questions.question: A table shows y-values 6, 12, 24, 48 when x = 0, 1, 2, 3. Which equation fits?
questions.explanation: The outputs double each step and start at 6 when x = 0, so y = 6·2^x.

questions.question: If 3^(x - 1) = 27, what is x ?
questions.explanation: Since 27 = 3^3, x - 1 = 3 and x = 4.

questions.question: Two quantities start at 100. A = 100(1.04)^t and B = 100 + 6t. For very large t, which statement is true?
questions.explanation: An exponential function eventually outgrows a linear function, so A will exceed B.

questions.question: An exponential function has y = 2 when x = 0 and y = 6 when x = 1. Which equation matches?
questions.explanation: The initial value is 2 and the growth factor is 6/2 = 3, so y = 2·3^x.

questions.question: If 81(1/3)^t = 3, what is t ?
If y = a·b^x and the value doubles whenever x increases by 1, what is b ?
questions.explanation: Doubling means the growth factor for each 1-step increase is 2, so b = 2.

questions.question: If 3^x = 20, between which two integers does x lie?
questions.explanation: Since 3^2 = 9 and 3^3 = 27, x must lie between 2 and 3.

questions.question: For y = 50(0.75)^t, after how many periods is y first less than 30?
questions.explanation: After 1 period the value is 37.5, after 2 periods it is 28.125, which is the first time below 30.

questions.question: If 2^x = 40, between which 2 integers does x lie?
questions.explanation: 2^5 = 32 and 2^6 = 64, so x lies between 5 and 6.

questions.question: A population is modeled by P = 1200(1.03)^t. What does the 1.03 represent?
questions.explanation: A factor of 1.03 means the amount keeps 100% and gains 3% each period.

topics.name: Transformations and Congruence
topics.alignment: Utah Aspire Plus Grade 9 / Secondary Math I geometry: rigid motions, dilations, congruence, and similarity foundations

questions.question: Translate the point (2, -1) by the vector ⟨3, 4⟩.
questions.explanation: Add 3 to the x-coordinate and 4 to the y-coordinate: (2 + 3, -1 + 4) = (5, 3).

questions.question: What is the image of (4, -2) after reflection over the x-axis?
questions.explanation: Reflection over the x-axis keeps x the same and changes the sign of y.

questions.question: Which transformation always preserves both side lengths and angle measures?
questions.explanation: A translation is a rigid motion, so it preserves lengths and angle measures.

questions.question: Translate the point (-3, 4) by the vector ⟨5, -2⟩.
questions.explanation: Add 5 to the x-coordinate and subtract 2 from the y-coordinate: (-3 + 5, 4 - 2) = (2, 2).

questions.question: What is the image of (-6, 1) after reflection over the y-axis?
questions.explanation: Reflection over the y-axis changes the sign of x and keeps y the same, so (-6, 1) becomes (6, 1).

questions.question: Which transformation does NOT always preserve side lengths?
questions.explanation: Translations, reflections, and rotations are rigid motions. A dilation changes side lengths unless the scale factor is 1.

questions.question: Rotate the point (3, 1) 90° counterclockwise about the origin.
questions.explanation: A 90° counterclockwise rotation sends (x, y) to (-y, x), so (3, 1) goes to (-1, 3).

questions.question: Dilate the point (2, -3) by a scale factor of 2 about the origin.
questions.explanation: Multiply each coordinate by 2: (2, -3) → (4, -6).

questions.question: If two polygons have the same shape and the same size, the polygons are:
questions.explanation: Congruent figures have the same size and shape.

questions.question: Rotate the point (-2, 5) 90° clockwise about the origin.
questions.explanation: A 90° clockwise rotation sends (x, y) to (y, -x). So (-2, 5) becomes (5, 2).

questions.question: Dilate the point (6, -8) by a scale factor of 1/2 about the origin.
questions.explanation: Multiply each coordinate by 1/2: (6, -8) → (3, -4).

questions.question: If two polygons are congruent, their corresponding side lengths are:
questions.explanation: Congruent figures have the same size and shape, so corresponding side lengths are equal.

questions.question: What is the image of the point (-5, 2) after reflection over the y-axis?
questions.explanation: Reflection over the y-axis changes the sign of x and keeps y the same.

questions.question: A point starts at (1, 2). It is translated right 4 units and then reflected over the x-axis. Where does it end up?
questions.explanation: After the translation the point is (5, 2). Reflecting over the x-axis gives (5, -2).

questions.question: A triangle is dilated by a scale factor of 3/2. How is its perimeter affected?
questions.explanation: All lengths in a dilation scale by the scale factor, so perimeter also scales by 3/2.

questions.question: What is the image of the point (-4, 7) after reflection over the x-axis?
questions.explanation: Reflection over the x-axis keeps x the same and changes the sign of y.

questions.question: A point starts at (-1, 3). It is translated left 2 units and then reflected over the y-axis. Where does it end up?
questions.explanation: After translating left 2 units, the point is (-3, 3). Reflecting over the y-axis gives (3, 3).

questions.question: A pentagon with perimeter 24 units is dilated by a scale factor of 5/4. What is the new perimeter?
questions.explanation: Perimeter scales by the same factor as side lengths: 24 × 5/4 = 30.

questions.question: A square is dilated by a scale factor of 4. By what factor does its area change?
questions.explanation: Area changes by the square of the scale factor, so 4^2 = 16.

questions.question: Which point is unchanged by reflection across the line y = x ?
questions.explanation: Points on the line y = x have equal coordinates, so (-3, -3) stays fixed.

questions.question: If one triangle can be translated and rotated to land exactly on another triangle, what can you conclude?
choices: The triangles are similar but not congruent
The triangles have equal area only
questions.explanation: A sequence of rigid motions mapping one figure onto another proves the figures are congruent.

questions.question: A figure is dilated by a scale factor of 1/3. By what factor does its area change?
questions.explanation: Area scales by the square of the scale factor. (1/3)^2 = 1/9.

questions.question: Which point is unchanged by reflection across the x-axis?
questions.explanation: A point on the x-axis has y = 0, so reflecting across the x-axis leaves it unchanged.

questions.question: If a quadrilateral can be mapped onto another quadrilateral by a sequence of translations, rotations, and reflections, what can you conclude?
choices: The quadrilaterals are similar but not congruent.
The quadrilaterals have the same area only.
The quadrilaterals must both be rectangles.
questions.explanation: A sequence of rigid motions preserves lengths and angles, so the figures are congruent.

questions.question: Rotate the point (-3, 5) 180° about the origin.
questions.explanation: A 180° rotation about the origin sends (x, y) to (-x, -y).

questions.question: A segment with endpoints A(1, 2) and B(4, 2) is moved to A'(3, 5) and B'(6, 5). Which translation maps the segment to its image?
questions.explanation: The x-values increase by 2 and the y-values increase by 3, so the rule is (x + 2, y + 3).

questions.question: Two similar triangles have a side-length ratio of 2:5 from the smaller to the larger. What is the ratio of their areas?
questions.explanation: Area scales by the square of the side-length ratio, so 2^2 : 5^2 = 4 : 25.

questions.question: Rotate the point (6, -2) 180° about the origin.
questions.explanation: A 180° rotation about the origin sends (x, y) to (-x, -y).

questions.question: A segment with endpoints A(-2, 1) and B(1, 1) is moved to A'(1, -3) and B'(4, -3). Which translation maps the segment to its image?
questions.explanation: The x-values increase by 3 and the y-values decrease by 4, so the rule is (x + 3, y - 4).

questions.question: Two similar rectangles have a side-length ratio of 3:4 from the smaller to the larger. What is the ratio of their areas?
questions.explanation: Area scales by the square of the side-length ratio, so 3^2 : 4^2 = 9 : 16.

topics.name: Geometry and Coordinate Geometry
topics.alignment: Utah Aspire Plus Grade 9 / Secondary Math I geometry and coordinate reasoning

questions.question: A rectangle is 5 units by 8 units. What is its perimeter?
questions.explanation: Perimeter = 2(5 + 8) = 26.

questions.question: What is the distance between the points (2, 3) and (2, 9)?
questions.explanation: The x-values are the same, so the distance is the difference in y-values: 9 - 3 = 6.

questions.question: If 2 angles are supplementary and one angle measures 65°, what is the measure of the other angle?
questions.explanation: Supplementary angles add to 180°, so the other angle is 180 - 65 = 115°.

questions.question: What is the area of a rectangle with length 8 and width 5?
questions.explanation: Area of a rectangle is length × width = 8 × 5 = 40.

questions.question: What is the circumference of a circle with radius 4?
questions.explanation: Circumference is 2πr, so 2π(4) = 8π.

questions.question: What is the distance between -3 and 7 on a number line?
questions.explanation: Distance on a number line is the absolute difference: |7 - (-3)| = 10.

questions.question: A triangle has angles measuring 50° and 60°. What is the measure of the third angle?
questions.explanation: Triangle angles add to 180°, so the third angle is 180 - 110 = 70°.

questions.question: What is the area of a circle with radius 4?
What is the midpoint of the segment with endpoints (2, 1) and (6, 5)?
questions.explanation: Average the x-values and y-values: ((2 + 6)/2, (1 + 5)/2) = (4, 3).

questions.question: A right triangle has legs 9 and 12. What is the length of the hypotenuse?
questions.explanation: Use the Pythagorean theorem: c = sqrt(9^2 + 12^2) = sqrt(81 + 144) = sqrt(225) = 15.

questions.question: What is the midpoint of the segment joining (2, 5) and (8, -1)?
questions.explanation: Average the x-coordinates and y-coordinates: ((2 + 8)/2, (5 + (-1))/2) = (5, 2).

questions.question: What is the slope of the line through (-1, 4) and (3, 6)?
questions.explanation: Compute the change in y over the change in x: (6 - 4)/(3 - (-1)) = 2/4 = 1/2.

questions.question: Two similar rectangles have a scale factor of 3:5 from the smaller to the larger. If the smaller rectangle has a side length of 12, what is the corresponding side length of the larger rectangle?
questions.explanation: Multiply by 5/3: 12·5/3 = 20.

questions.question: Which equation represents a circle centered at the origin with radius 6?
questions.explanation: For a circle centered at the origin, the equation is x^2 + y^2 = r^2.

questions.question: A right triangle has legs of lengths 9 and 12. What is the length of the hypotenuse?
questions.explanation: Use the Pythagorean theorem: √(9^2 + 12^2) = √225 = 15.

questions.question: What is the area of a triangle with base 10 and height 7?
questions.explanation: Area of a triangle is (1/2)bh = (1/2)(10)(7) = 35.

questions.question: Find the distance between (1, 2) and (4, 6).
questions.explanation: Use the distance formula: sqrt((4 - 1)^2 + (6 - 2)^2) = sqrt(3^2 + 4^2) = 5.

questions.question: Which equation represents a circle centered at the origin with radius 5?
questions.explanation: A circle with center (0, 0) and radius r has equation x^2 + y^2 = r^2.

questions.question: What is the area of a trapezoid with bases 8 and 14 and height 5?
What is the distance between (-1, 2) and (3, 5)?
A line passes through (2, -1) and is parallel to y = 3x + 4. Which equation could represent the line?
questions.explanation: Parallel lines have the same slope 3. Using point (2, -1): -1 = 3(2) + b, so b = -7.

questions.question: What is the volume of a cylinder with radius 3 and height 10?
questions.explanation: Volume of a cylinder is πr^2h = π(3^2)(10) = 90π.

questions.question: What is the surface area of a rectangular prism with dimensions 3, 4, and 5?
questions.explanation: Surface area is 2(lw + lh + wh) = 2(12 + 15 + 20) = 94.

questions.question: Which equation represents the line through (2, -1) with slope -3?
questions.explanation: Use point-slope form: y + 1 = -3(x - 2). Simplify to y = -3x + 5.

questions.question: What is the volume of a cylinder with radius 3 and height 8?
questions.explanation: Volume = πr^2h = π(3^2)(8) = 72π.

questions.question: A 10-foot ladder leans against a wall and reaches 8 feet high. How far is the bottom of the ladder from the wall?
questions.explanation: Use a right triangle with hypotenuse 10 and leg 8: x = √(10^2 - 8^2) = √36 = 6.

questions.question: Which point lies on the graph of y = -x^2 + 4x + 5 ?
questions.explanation: When x = 2, y = -(2^2) + 4(2) + 5 = -4 + 8 + 5 = 9, so (2, 9) lies on the graph.

questions.question: Two similar figures have side-length ratio 2:5. If the larger figure has area 75, what is the area of the smaller figure?
questions.explanation: Areas scale by the square of the side ratio. The area ratio is 4:25, so the smaller area is (4/25)(75) = 12.

questions.question: What is the area of the triangle with vertices (0, 0), (6, 0), and (2, 4)?
questions.explanation: Use the base along the x-axis: base = 6 and height = 4. Area is (1/2)(6)(4) = 12.

questions.question: Point P lies on the perpendicular bisector of segment AB. Which statement must be true?
choices: P is the midpoint of AB
A, B, and P are collinear
questions.explanation: Every point on the perpendicular bisector of a segment is equidistant from the segment's endpoints.

topics.name: Statistics, Probability, and Data
topics.alignment: Utah Aspire Plus Grade 9 / Secondary Math I statistics and linear modeling foundations

questions.question: What is the mean of 4, 6, and 8?
A bag contains 3 red chips and 2 blue chips. If 1 chip is chosen at random, what is the probability of choosing a red chip?
questions.explanation: There are 3 favorable outcomes out of 5 total outcomes.

questions.question: What is the median of 2, 7, 9, 10, 14?
questions.explanation: The middle value of the ordered list is 9.

questions.question: What is the probability of rolling an even number on a fair six-sided die?
questions.explanation: Even outcomes are 2, 4, and 6, so there are 3 favorable outcomes out of 6 total: 3/6 = 1/2.

questions.question: What is the mean of 4, 7, and 9?
questions.explanation: Add the values to get 20, then divide by 3.

questions.question: What is the median of 3, 8, 8, 10, and 12?
questions.explanation: The values are already ordered, and the middle value is 8.

questions.question: A table shows the number of books sold on 4 days: Monday 12, Tuesday 15, Wednesday 9, Thursday 14. On which day were the most books sold?
questions.explanation: The greatest value in the table is 15 on Tuesday.

questions.question: A spinner has 8 equal sections: 3 are green and 5 are yellow. What is the probability of landing on yellow?
questions.explanation: There are 5 yellow sections out of 8 total sections.

questions.question: What is the range of the data set 18, 23, 19, 30 ?
questions.explanation: Range = greatest value - least value = 30 - 18 = 12.

questions.question: In a survey, 30 students like red jelly beans, 29 like green, and 17 like both. If 50 students were surveyed, how many like neither color?
questions.explanation: Use inclusion-exclusion: 30 + 29 - 17 = 42 students like at least one color. Then 50 - 42 = 8 like neither.

questions.question: What is the probability of drawing a heart from a standard deck of 52 cards?
questions.explanation: There are 13 hearts in a 52-card deck, so the probability is 13/52 = 1/4.

questions.question: What is the range of the data set 12, 5, 19, 8?
questions.explanation: Range is largest minus smallest: 19 - 5 = 14.

questions.question: A scatterplot shows that as study time increases, test score also tends to increase. What type of association does the scatterplot show?
questions.explanation: If both variables tend to increase together, the association is positive.

questions.question: In a group of 20 students, 12 play a sport, 7 play an instrument, and 4 do both. How many do neither?
questions.explanation: Students who do at least 1 activity: 12 + 7 - 4 = 15. So neither = 20 - 15 = 5.

questions.question: What is the mean of the scores 80, 90, 70, and 100?
A bag contains 5 red, 3 blue, and 2 green marbles. What is the probability of drawing a marble that is not red?
questions.explanation: There are 5 marbles that are not red out of 10 total, so the probability is 5/10 = 1/2.

questions.question: A student scored 85, 73, 90, and 77 on four tests. What score is needed on the next test for an average of 82?
questions.explanation: Set up (85 + 73 + 90 + 77 + x)/5 = 82. The known sum is 325, so 325 + x = 410 and x = 85.

questions.question: What is the mode of the data set 2, 5, 5, 7, 9, 9, 9, 10?
questions.explanation: The mode is the value that occurs most often. Here 9 appears three times.

questions.question: A data set has values 5, 6, 6, 7, 30. Which measure of center is affected more by the outlier 30?
questions.explanation: The mean is pulled strongly by large outliers; the median is less affected.

questions.question: What is the probability of getting at least 1 head when 2 fair coins are flipped?
questions.explanation: The only way not to get at least 1 head is TT, which has probability 1/4. So the answer is 1 - 1/4 = 3/4.

questions.question: A line of best fit for a data set is y = 2.5x + 4. What value does the line predict when x = 6?
What is the probability of getting exactly two heads in three fair coin tosses?
questions.explanation: The favorable outcomes are HHT, HTH, and THH, so there are 3 favorable outcomes out of 8 total outcomes.

questions.question: How many different 2-person committees can be chosen from 5 students?
questions.explanation: Use combinations: C(5, 2) = 5·4 / (2·1) = 10.

questions.question: A data set has the value 1 appearing 2 times, 2 appearing 3 times, and 3 appearing 1 time. What is the mean?
questions.explanation: Compute the weighted sum: 1·2 + 2·3 + 3·1 = 11. There are 2 + 3 + 1 = 6 values, so the mean is 11/6.

questions.question: Which sampling method is most likely to produce an unbiased estimate of the average number of hours students sleep each night?
choices: Ask only students in the library after midnight
Ask every tenth student entering school in the morning
Ask only members of the soccer team
Post an optional poll on social media
questions.correctChoice: Ask every tenth student entering school in the morning
questions.explanation: Selecting every tenth student entering school is more representative than sampling only a special subgroup or volunteers.

questions.question: A box contains 5 math books and 3 science books. Two books are selected at random without replacement. What is the probability that both are science books?
questions.explanation: The probability is (3/8)(2/7) = 6/56 = 3/28.

questions.question: A game pays $5 with probability 0.2 and $0 otherwise. What is the expected value of 1 play?

In a club, 12 economics majors and 9 business majors are members. Outside the club, there are 8 economics majors and 11 business majors. If a student is known to be an economics major, what is the probability the student is in the club?
questions.explanation: Among economics majors, 12 are in the club and 8 are not, so there are 20 economics majors total. The conditional probability is 12/20 = 3/5.

questions.question: What is the probability of rolling at least one 6 in two rolls of a fair die?
questions.explanation: Use the complement. The probability of no 6 on either roll is (5/6)^2 = 25/36, so the probability of at least one 6 is 1 - 25/36 = 11/36.

questions.question: A data set has mean 20. If every data value is increased by 5, what is the new mean?
questions.explanation: Adding the same amount to every data value increases the mean by that amount, so the mean becomes 25.
